description: Peer-reviewed authors whose published work often diverges from mainstream or big-pharma consensus. Inclusion requires verifiable peer-reviewed publication history (Tier 1 or Tier 2 from practitioner tiers research). Tier 3/4 names are intentionally excluded because they have no peer-reviewed work for PULSE to cross-reference.

tier_1_extensive.name: Peter A. McCullough
tier_1_extensive.notes: Extensive cardiology and CKD research. Controversial positions on COVID-era therapeutics.

tier_1_extensive.name: David A. Sinclair
tier_1_extensive.notes: Harvard Medical School. 109K+ citations. Longevity claims sometimes challenged by mainstream gerontology.

tier_1_extensive.name: James J. DiNicolantonio
tier_1_extensive.notes: Saint Luke's Mid America Heart Institute. Published on magnesium, omega-3, salt. LENA showcase researcher.

tier_1_extensive.name: Robert W. Malone
tier_1_extensive.notes: Early mRNA platform contributor. 11K+ citations. Dissenting positions on mRNA vaccine policy.

tier_1_extensive.name: Andrew D. Huberman
tier_1_extensive.notes: Stanford. Published in Nature, Science, Cell.

tier_2_some_publications.name: Mary Talley Bowden
tier_2_some_publications.notes: Peer-reviewed work is in ENT/sleep, not her public advocacy areas. Flag this context when surfacing.

tier_2_some_publications.name: Deborah Murtagh
tier_2_some_publications.notes: Co-authored ketogenic diet research in neurology.

excluded_from_outlier_list.reason: No verifiable peer-reviewed publication history in their current public advocacy areas. Including them would violate LENA agent rules (never guess, score only on cited facts). They are excluded from the outlier filter entirely.
